# Billsby API

**OpenAPI spec included** · [View spec →](./1.0.0/openapi.json) · *Generated by Jentic — the vendor does not publish an official OpenAPI specification.*

Manage subscription customers, plans, invoices, add-ons, and allowances for recurring billing across 58 endpoints.

| | |
|---|---|
| **Base URL** | `https://public.billsby.com/api/v1/{companyDomain}` |
| **Auth** | API Key |
| **Endpoints** | 58 |

## What you can build with this API

- Enroll customers in subscription plans with configurable billing cycles and trial periods
- Upgrade, downgrade, or cancel subscriptions mid-cycle with prorated adjustments
- Attach add-ons and metered allowances to subscriptions for usage-based pricing
- Retrieve invoice history and payment logs per customer for accounting reconciliation
- Enforce GDPR data clearing on customer records while preserving billing integrity

Does not handle payment gateway integration or direct card processing — use it for subscription lifecycle management on top of your existing payment provider.

## Key endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | /customers | Create a customer |
| GET | /customers/{customerId}/subscriptions | Get customer subscriptions |
| POST | /customers/{customerId}/charge | Create a one-time charge |
| GET | /customers/{customerId}/paymentlogs | Get payment logs |
| GET | /products | List products and plans |
| PUT | /customers/{customerId}/gdpr | Clear customer data (GDPR) |

## Key resources

| Resource | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Customers | Create, retrieve, and manage subscriber accounts and payment details |
| Subscriptions | Enroll, modify, cancel, and query subscription status |
| Invoices | Retrieve and manage billing invoices and payment history |
| Products | Define products, plans, cycles, and pricing tiers |
| Add-Ons | Attach optional add-on charges to subscriptions |
| Custom Fields | Collect and manage additional customer data fields |

### Python quickstart

Three steps: search for what you need, load the operation schema, execute. Your agent calls this API through Jentic without touching API keys — credentials are injected server-side at runtime.

```python
import asyncio
from jentic import Jentic, SearchRequest, LoadRequest, ExecutionRequest

async def main():
    client = Jentic()
    search = await client.search(SearchRequest(query="create a subscription customer and assign a billing plan"))
    entity_id = search.results[0].id
    details = await client.load(LoadRequest(ids=[entity_id]))
    result = await client.execute(
        ExecutionRequest(
            id=entity_id,
            inputs={"firstName": "Jane", "lastName": "Doe", "email": "jane@example.com", "companyDomain": "acme"}
        )
    )
    print(result)

asyncio.run(main())
```

Works with LangChain, CrewAI, OpenAI Agents SDK, Claude Agent SDK, and any MCP-compatible framework.
